title: ochlandra keralensis
intro: Ochlandra keralensis, also known as the Indian staff tree or Kalakam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ochlandra keralensis include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and cold, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. In traditional medicine, Ochlandra keralensis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, which can be taken orally or applied topically to treat various health conditions.
